segments.text: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread, called the Passover, was approaching,
and the chief priests and scribes were looking for a way to put Jesus to death; for they feared the people.
Then Satan entered Judas Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
And Judas went to discuss with the chief priests and temple officers how he might betray Jesus to them.
They were delighted and agreed to give him money.
Judas consented, and began to look for an opportunity to betray Jesus to them in the absence of a crowd.
Then came the day of Unleavened Bread on which the Passover lamb was to be sacrificed.
Jesus sent Peter and John, saying,
Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Where do You want us to prepare it?
segments.speaker: Peter and John

segments.text: they asked.
He answered,
When you enter the city, a man carrying a jug of water will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and say to the owner of that house, ‘The Teacher asks: Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with My disciples?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And he will show you a large upper room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they went and found it just as Jesus had told them. And they prepared the Passover.
When the hour had come, Jesus reclined at the table with His apostles.
And He said to them,
I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before My suffering.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I tell you that I will not eat it again until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After taking the cup, He gave thanks and said,
Take this and divide it among yourselves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I tell you that I will not drink of the fruit of the vine from now on until the kingdom of God comes.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And He took the bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to them, saying,
This is My body, given for you; do this in remembrance of Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the same way, after supper He took the cup, saying,
This cup is the new covenant in My blood, which is poured out for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Look! The hand of My betrayer is with Mine on the table.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Indeed, the Son of Man will go as it has been determined, but woe to that man who betrays Him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then they began to question among themselves which of them was going to do this.
A dispute also arose among the disciples as to which of them would be considered the greatest.
So Jesus declared,
The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those in authority over them call themselves benefactors.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But you shall not be like them. Instead, the greatest among you should be like the youngest, and the one who leads like the one who serves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For who is greater, the one who reclines at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one who reclines? But I am among you as one who serves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You are the ones who have stood by Me in My trials.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And I bestow on you a kingdom, just as My Father has bestowed one on Me,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: so that you may eat and drink at My table in My kingdom, and sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Simon, Simon, Satan has asked to sift each of you like wheat.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith will not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord,
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: said Peter,
I am ready to go with You even to prison and to death.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: But Jesus replied,
I tell you, Peter, the rooster will not crow today until you have denied three times that you know Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus asked them,
When I sent you out without purse or bag or sandals, did you lack anything?
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: Nothing,
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: they answered.
Now, however,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He told them,
the one with a purse should take it, and likewise a bag; and the one without a sword should sell his cloak and buy one.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I tell you that this Scripture must be fulfilled in Me: ‘And He was numbered with the transgressors.’ For what is written about Me is reaching its fulfillment.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they said,
Look, Lord, here are two swords.
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: That is enough,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He answered.

text: Jesus Prays on the Mount of Olives

segments.text: Jesus went out as usual to the Mount of Olives, and the disciples followed Him.
When He came to the place, He told them,
Pray that you will not enter into temptation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And He withdrew about a stone’s throw beyond them, where He knelt down and prayed,
Father, if You are willing, take this cup from Me. Yet not My will, but Yours be done.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then an angel from heaven appeared to Him and strengthened Him.
And in His anguish, He prayed more earnestly, and His sweat became like drops of blood falling to the ground.
When Jesus rose from prayer and returned to the disciples, He found them asleep, exhausted from sorrow.
Why are you sleeping?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He asked.
Get up and pray so that you will not enter into temptation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While He was still speaking, a crowd arrived, led by the man called Judas, one of the Twelve. He approached Jesus to kiss Him.
But Jesus asked him,
Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those around Jesus saw what was about to happen and said,
Lord, should we strike with our swords?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And one of them struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
But Jesus answered,
No more of this!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And He touched the man’s ear and healed him.
Then Jesus said to the chief priests, temple officers, and elders who had come for Him,
Have you come out with swords and clubs as you would against an outlaw?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Every day I was with you in the temple courts, and you did not lay a hand on Me. But this hour belongs to you and to the power of darkness.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then they seized Jesus, led Him away, and took Him into the house of the high priest. And Peter followed at a distance.
When those present had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat down among them.
A servant girl saw him seated in the firelight and looked intently at him.
This man also was with Him,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: she said.
But Peter denied it.
Woman, I do not know Him,
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: he said.
A short time later, someone else saw him and said,
You also are one of them.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: But Peter said,
Man, I am not.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: About an hour later, another man insisted,
Certainly this man was with Him, for he too is a Galilean.
segments.speaker: About an hour later, another man

segments.text: Man, I do not know what you are talking about,
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text:
Peter replied.
While he was still speaking, the rooster crowed.

And the Lord turned and looked at Peter.
Then Peter remembered the word that the Lord had spoken to him:
Before the rooster crows today, you will deny Me three times.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: And he went outside and wept bitterly.
The men who were holding Jesus began to mock Him and beat Him.
They blindfolded Him and kept demanding,
Prophesy! Who hit You?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: And they said many other blasphemous things against Him.
At daybreak the council of the elders of the people, both the chief priests and scribes, met together. They led Jesus into their Sanhedrin and said,
If You are the Christ, tell us.
segments.speaker: Chief Priests and Elders

segments.text: Jesus answered,
If I tell you, you will not believe.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And if I ask you a question, you will not answer.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they all asked,
Are You then the Son of God?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He replied,
You say that I am.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Why do we need any more testimony?
segments.speaker: Chief Priests and Elders

segments.text: they declared.
We have heard it for ourselves from His own lips.
segments.speaker: Unknown
